news.headline: Crows hold off Cats in thriller for stunning win
news.body: The Crows have overcome the Cats in an enthralling clash.
news.ai_summary: Jordan Dawson (Adelaide) was best afield with 22 disposals and 3 goals in a captain's knock, making him a hold/POD target despite the modest possession count. Young gun Dan Curtin impressed with workrate and a game-saving mark, while recruit Callum Ah Chee tagged Tom Stewart to just 15 disposals and 3 intercepts before Stewart left with a hamstring injury — a major concern for Stewart owners. Adelaide also lost Jordon Butts and Ah Chee to injury, while Taylor Walker may face MRO scrutiny for a shove on Connor O'Sullivan. Ben Keays and debutant Hugo Hall-Kahan (head clash with Toby Murray) are also worth monitoring.

news.headline: Coach defends doctors as Crows players play out game after head clash
news.body: Matthew Nicks defends his medical staff after two Crows were cleared to play out their win against the Cats without a concussion test after a nasty head clash.
news.ai_summary: Adelaide's Callum Ah Chee (hamstring – his fourth since joining the Crows; 11 disposals, 1 goal) and Jordon Butts (calf) both failed to finish the one-point win over Geelong, with the extent unknown – owners should brace for outs. Debutant Hugo Hall-Kahan and Toby Murray passed concussion checks after a head clash and returned to play. Taylor Walker (2 goals on return) faces MRO scrutiny for shoving Connor O'Sullivan, risking a suspension. Skipper Jordan Dawson was the standout with 22 disposals and 3 goals despite a sore hip, while Patrick Dangerfield posted a season-high 30 disposals and 7 clearances in the loss.

news.headline: Crows, Cats lock in final teams for huge clash. LIVE from 7pm ACST
news.body: Follow all the action from Thursday night's clash between Adelaide and Geelong.
news.ai_summary: Geelong have recalled Jack Bowes, Jack Martin and Ollie Wiltshire for the Thursday night clash with Adelaide, with Lawson Humphries (ankle) out, Mitch Edwards managed and Brad Close dropped — denting the job security of Cats-owned mid-pricers. Adelaide bring back Taylor Walker and hand a debut to mid-season recruit Hugo Hall-Kahan, while Izak Rankine (calf) and Riley Thilthorpe (suspension) are notable fantasy outs. Rankine owners face a hit to scoring with no late changes available, while Hall-Kahan looms as a cheap cash-cow option for those chasing rookie value.

news.headline: F.O'Sullivan OUT: North Melbourne — Concussion
news.body: F.O'Sullivan has been ruled OUT for North Melbourne in this round. Reason: concussion.
news.ai_summary: North Melbourne youngster Finn O'Sullivan is OUT for Round 13 after entering concussion protocols, ruling him unavailable against Fremantle and creating a likely donut risk for Fantasy coaches holding him as a rookie. Zane Fisher comes in as his direct replacement. Fremantle, meanwhile, regains Hayden Young from concussion alongside Oscar McDonald and Jaeger O'Meara, a boost for Young owners after his recent absences. Trade urgency applies if O'Sullivan is on your field with no loophole option available.

news.headline: B.Cox OUT: Fremantle — Knee
news.body: B.Cox has been ruled OUT for Fremantle in this round. Reason: knee.
news.ai_summary: Fremantle's Brennan Cox is OUT with a knee injury for Saturday's clash with North Melbourne, alongside Matthew Johnson (hamstring), opening the door for Oscar McDonald, Hayden Young (returning from concussion) and Josh O'Meara. Young's return is the key fantasy-relevant inclusion for Dockers owners. North Melbourne lose Finn O'Sullivan (concussion), with Zane Fisher coming in. Cox owners will need to trade or bench him given the indefinite knee setback.

news.headline: M.Johnson OUT: Fremantle — Hamstring
news.body: M.Johnson has been ruled OUT for Fremantle in this round. Reason: hamstring.
news.ai_summary: Fremantle's Michael Johnson is OUT with a hamstring injury for the round 13 clash against North Melbourne, forcing coaches to trade or bench him. The Dockers regain Hayden Young (concussion) and also bring in Oliver McDonald and Jaeger O'Meara, with Bailey Cox (knee) also sidelined. North Melbourne lose young gun Finn O'Sullivan to concussion protocols, with Zane Fisher coming in. Hamstring setbacks make Johnson a hold-or-trade call depending on your bench cover.

news.headline: A.May OUT: Essendon — Shoulder
news.body: A.May has been ruled OUT for Essendon in this round. Reason: shoulder.
news.ai_summary: Essendon defender Aaron May is OUT for Sunday night's clash against Carlton with a shoulder injury, forcing coaches holding him to find a replacement or accept a donut. Sam Durham and Mason Redman return for the Bombers, while Darcy Parish (knee soreness) is also sidelined — a blow for owners given his midfield ceiling. Other key fantasy-relevant outs include Brisbane's Hugh McCluggage (calf) and Port Adelaide's Kane Farrell (adductor), while Fremantle welcomes back Hayden Young from concussion and Sydney regains Callum Mills and Brodie Grundy.

news.headline: Z.Uwland OUT: Gold Coast — Omitted

Brisbane meanwhile is decimated, losing co-captain Hugh McCluggage (calf) on top of Keidean Coleman (hamstring), with Darcy Joyce, Jaspa Tunstill and Sam Brain recalled.

news.ai_summary: Selection alert: Brandon Starcevich will debut for West Coast against Port Adelaide on Saturday night, adding a two-time Brisbane premiership defender to fantasy defensive options. Captain Liam Duggan continues to anchor the backline as the Eagles improve to 4-8, with young guns Harley Reid, Reuben Ginbey, Willem Duursma, Cooper Duff-Tytler and Bo Allan all relevant fantasy watches amid improving team form. With winnable matches against Port Adelaide and North Melbourne in Perth before the bye, scoring opportunities should rise for West Coast mids and defenders, potentially trimming break-evens across the board.

Caleb Serong misses a third game with a calf, and Brennan Cox (knee) may be rested with next week's bye, opening a likely recall for Oscar McDonald. Sean Darcy and Jaeger O'Meara will spend another week in the WAFL, delaying any DPP/ruck owner returns. North Melbourne tagger Finn O'Sullivan is out for two weeks with concussion, removing his tagging threat.

news.headline: Young set to return but Dockers to play it safe with other key players
news.body: Justin Longmuir will take a conservative path with several important players coming back from injury.
news.ai_summary: Hayden Young (Fremantle) returns from concussion this week vs North Melbourne, and Matthew Johnson is cleared after a hamstring scare — both back into fantasy sides post-bye consideration. However, Caleb Serong misses a third straight game with a calf, while Brennan Cox (knee) may be rested with the bye looming, opening a recall for Oscar McDonald. Sean Darcy and Jaeger O'Meara will spend another week in the WAFL, so hold off on ruck/mid trades targeting them. North Melbourne tagger Finn O'Sullivan is out for two weeks with concussion, freeing up Fremantle's mid scoring.

news.headline: Lions go back to basics in bid to break out of slump ahead of QClash
news.body: Chris Fagan has told Brisbane's leaders to worry about their own game first as they seek to lead the premiers back to winning form.
news.ai_summary: Brisbane co-captains Hugh McCluggage and Josh Dunkley remain well below their fantasy ceilings as the Lions chase a turnaround in Saturday's QClash against Gold Coast, with coach Chris Fagan urging his leaders to refocus on their own games. Key defender Harris Andrews also had a rare quiet outing on Fremantle's Josh Treacy, while Cam Rayner, Will Ashcroft, Jaspa Fletcher and Jarrod Berry have all been down on output. Oscar Allen remains sidelined through injury. With Fagan preaching "simplicity" and more freedom, owners of McCluggage, Dunkley and Ashcroft will be hoping for a bounce-back as break-evens climb.

news.headline: Josh Worrell: New Crow told to play on instinct, with a familiar face by his side
news.body: New Crow told to play on instinct, with a familiar face by his side.
news.ai_summary: Adelaide mid-season draftee Hugo Hall-Kahan will debut against Geelong on Thursday night as a rebounding half-back, just nine days after being drafted — a rookie-priced option worth monitoring after averaging 27.7 disposals in the VFL. He'll line up alongside close mate Josh Worrell, with Wayne Milera (team-high 26.3 disposals) the established attacking driver from defence and an All-Australian chance. Tall defender Mark Keane returns via the SANFL this weekend from a lower-leg fracture, threatening to squeeze Crows defensive job security once he's recalled.

news.headline: New Crow told to play on instinct, with a familiar face by his side
news.body: Hugo Hall-Kahan has been backed to make an immediate impact at AFL level.
news.ai_summary: Adelaide mid-season draftee Hugo Hall-Kahan will debut against Geelong on Thursday night as a rebounding half-back, just nine days after being recruited. He averaged 27.7 disposals in the VFL with Williamstown and looms as a cheap rookie option, slotting alongside close mate Josh Worrell and complementing Wayne Milera (team-high 26.3 disposals). Tall defender Mark Keane also returns via the SANFL this weekend, firming as a backline option once match-fit. Milera remains the key fantasy hold from Adelaide's defence amid All-Australian form.

news.headline: Tom Stewart: 'Thorn in a few sides': Star Cat looms large in Crows' planning
news.body: 'Thorn in a few sides': Star Cat looms large in Crows' planning.
news.ai_summary: Geelong's Tom Stewart shapes as a major matchup concern for Adelaide on Thursday night, having racked up nine intercept possessions and a goal in the round three eight-point win — a reminder of his intercept ceiling for SuperCoach defenders. Adelaide forward Darcy Fogarty returns to full fitness after a bulging disc, boosting his forward-line scoring outlook. The Crows lose Riley Thilthorpe (suspension) and Izak Rankine (calf), with Taylor Walker recalled and mid-season draftee Hugo Hall-Kahan debuting as a rookie-priced defender option.

news.headline: M.Edwards OUT: Geelong — Managed
news.body: M.Edwards has been ruled OUT for Geelong in this round. Reason: managed.
news.ai_summary: Geelong ruck Mitch Edwards has been managed for Thursday night's clash with Adelaide, alongside omitted half-back Lawson Humphries (ankle) and Brad Close (omitted), with Jack Bowes, Jack Martin and Ollie Wiltshire coming in. Humphries' absence is notable for fantasy defenders given his strong recent form, while Close owners cop another donut. For Adelaide, Izak Rankine (calf) and Riley Thilthorpe (suspension) are out, replaced by Taylor Walker and mid-season draftee Hugo Hall-Kahan, who debuts as a running defender after averaging 27.7 disposals in the VFL.

news.headline: 'Thorn in a few sides': Star Cat looms large in Crows' planning
news.body: Tom Stewart cut up Adelaide when they last played Geelong and Crows forward Darcy Fogarty said the star defender has been a focus of their planning.
news.ai_summary: Adelaide will target Geelong's Tom Stewart, who racked up 9 intercept possessions and a goal in the Cats' 8-point round 3 win, with the Crows planning to kick away from him — a negative sign for Stewart's intercept-reliant SuperCoach scoring. Taylor Walker returns from injury to replace the suspended Riley Thilthorpe, while Izak Rankine is out with a calf, opening up forward minutes. Mid-season draftee Hugo Hall-Kahan (22yo defender) debuts as a cheap rookie option. Darcy Fogarty backs up after returning from a bulging disc in the past fortnight.

news.headline: Dee weighing up decision, Hawk close to new deal, Dog to sign on
news.body: Dee weighing up decision, Hawk close to new deal, Dog to sign on.
news.ai_summary: Melbourne free agent Tom Sparrow will delay his contract decision until after the Demons' round 16 bye, with the 2021 premiership midfielder likely to be offered a four-year deal after taking on increased midfield duties — a role boost relevant for SuperCoach owners. Fellow Demons Kade Chandler and Bayley Fritsch are also in re-signing talks, suggesting role stability at Melbourne. Hawthorn's Jarman Impey is closing on an extension through 2029 off the back of an All-Australian-calibre half-back campaign, reinforcing his fantasy job security. Western Bulldogs defender Michael Sellwood is set to sign a two-year extension through 2028 after playing every game this season, locking in his backline role.

news.headline: 'Powerful': Daniher family speaks to Demons, Magpies
news.body: Neale Daniher's wife Jan and daughter Bec spoke to the Pies and Demons at the MCG.
news.ai_summary: Melbourne defender Jake Lever is confirmed to return from concussion for Monday's King's Birthday clash against Collingwood, ending his absence and giving Demons owners a lockdown defender back in their lineup. Melbourne forward Brody Mihocek faces his former club Collingwood for the first time, with Magpies defender Isaac Quaynor flagging he'll tag him closely around goal — a potential handbrake on Mihocek's scoring. With Melbourne losing its past two and Collingwood dropping three of its past four, both sides need the four points, making this a key matchup for fantasy-relevant mids and forwards on both lists.

news.headline: 'Never felt more settled': Gun midfielder loving life at the Swans
news.body: Chad Warner says he's loving life at the Swans since signing a two-year extension.
news.ai_summary: Sydney midfielder Chad Warner returned to form against Richmond with 3 goals and 2 assists after a quiet fortnight, alongside Isaac Heeney's monster outing (32 disposals, 13 score involvements, 5 goals) — both worth monitoring as buy-back options. Ruck Brodie Grundy returns this week after being rested, restoring his ceiling for SuperCoach owners, while Hayden McLean (4 goals) faces the axe with the talls all firing. Selection headaches loom with Callum Mills (calf), Matt Roberts (groin), Justin McInerney (ankle) and Malcolm Rosas jnr (ankle) all being assessed for Sunday's St Kilda clash — cover may be needed for Mills and Roberts owners.

news.headline: 'What bye?': Mitchell urges Hawks to focus on Bont, Bulldogs ahead of break
news.body: While Hawthorn will start favourite at the MCG, the Western Bulldogs have won three of their last four and will fancy their chances.
news.ai_summary: Selection boost for Hawthorn: Will Day returned successfully last week, while Tom Barrass (hamstring) and Conor Nash (neck spasms) are nearing recalls ahead of Friday night's clash with the Western Bulldogs. The Bulldogs welcome back ruckman Tim English, restoring his ceiling for owners after a four-game skid. Coach Sam Mitchell highlighted Marcus Bontempelli, Ed Richards, Ryley Sanders and Matt Kennedy as key Bulldogs midfield threats, all relevant for Fantasy scoring. Nick Watson also looms as a risk after his disallowed goal highlighted role/rule uncertainty.

news.headline: Docker O'Driscoll says mental health admission 'best thing' he's done
news.body: Fremantle Dockers midfielder Nathan O'Driscoll says he has received "nothing but love" after posting on social media about his struggles with anxiety and how he contemplated taking his own life.
news.ai_summary: Fremantle Dockers midfielder Nathan O'Driscoll is back in the senior side after speaking publicly about his mental health struggles, having played five games this season from 43 career AFL matches. His return to selection is a watch for fantasy coaches considering cheap Dockers mid options, though role and job security remain the key questions given his limited 2025 output. No updated price, average or break-even figures were provided in the article.

news.headline: Star defender unsure about Origin future, confident of strong Giants finish
news.body: Sam Taylor is confident the Giants can make a charge to finish the season, but whether he would play Origin again remains to be seen.
news.ai_summary: Sam Taylor (GWS Giants) returned to football on Sunday in the 49-point win over Melbourne, 15 weeks after a high-grade hamstring strain suffered in AAMI AFL Origin for Western Australia. He came to the bench multiple times clutching the hamstring but confirmed it was just cramp in the Alice Springs heat, easing fears for fantasy holders. With 11 games left and Taylor backing the Giants to win most, he's a hold as a defensive rock, though monitor his minutes in coming weeks before trusting him as a key defender option.

news.headline: Tom Green: Giants table monster offer to star mid as cashed-up Devils loom
news.body: Giants table monster offer to star mid as cashed-up Devils loom.
news.ai_summary: Tom Green is unavailable for all of 2025 after a February ACL reconstruction, ruling the Greater Western Sydney midfielder out as a fantasy option this season. The Giants have tabled a seven-year, ~$9 million extension through 2034 to fend off Tasmania, signalling his long-term role as a midfield cornerstone and likely future captain. The 2025 best and fairest winner remains a premium keeper target for 2026 once fully rehabbed, with potential discount pricing depending on his recovery timeline.

news.headline: Bombers seek special assistance package from AFL
news.body: Essendon has followed the lead of North Melbourne, Gold Coast and West Coast in asking for an assistance package to accelerate its return to competitiveness.
news.ai_summary: Essendon has opened talks with the AFL for an end-of-season assistance package after winning just one of its past 25 games and sitting last on the ladder, following the precedent set by West Coast, North Melbourne and Gold Coast. For fantasy coaches, this signals a likely influx of draft picks and expanded rookie list spots at Tullamarine, potentially squeezing job security for fringe Bombers while opening rookie-priced opportunities in 2026. Short term, the injury-ravaged list that frustrated sacked coach Brad Scott remains a problem under interim Dean Solomon after the 30-point loss to West Coast, so tread carefully with Essendon-exposed cash cows. No specific player prices, averages or break-evens were provided in the article.

news.headline: Rebuilding Richmond: Tigers president on Yze, injury crisis, Waverley move, eyeing India
news.body: John O'Rourke speaks to Cal Twomey on the big issues at Richmond amid a strategic rebuild at one of the AFL's giants. Status updated: Status NEWS → INJURY TBC.
news.ai_summary: Richmond president John O'Rourke has reaffirmed coach Adem Yze (9 wins from 58 games, contracted to 2028) amid a long-term rebuild, signalling continued pain for Tigers-owned fantasy assets after a 114-point loss to Sydney. The injury crisis to key young Tigers — Sam Lalor, Josh Gibcus, Taj Hotton, Tom Sims, Josh Smillie, Jonty Faull and Harry Armstrong — is wrecking job security and breakeven value, with no quick returns flagged. With O'Rourke conceding a 6-7 year rebuild, coaches should be wary of Richmond mid-pricers and instead target cheap rookies if/when these kids finally debut or return. A Punt Road redevelopment and possible Waverley/Gosch's Paddock relocation adds further disruption heading into 2026-27.

news.headline: Tom Doedee — OUT: Calf (6 weeks)
news.body: Tom Doedee has been ruled OUT with a calf Estimated return: 6 weeks.
news.ai_summary: Brisbane Lions defender Tom Doedee is sidelined for 6 weeks with a calf injury — trade him out now and free up a key defender slot.

news.headline: Lucas Camporeale — OUT: Kidney (2 weeks)
news.body: Lucas Camporeale has been ruled OUT with a kidney Estimated return: 2 weeks.
news.ai_summary: Carlton's Lucas Camporeale is OUT for 2 weeks with a kidney injury. Fantasy coaches should bench or trade him out until his return.

news.headline: Noah Howes — OUT: Shoulder (4-5 weeks)
news.body: Noah Howes has been ruled OUT with a shoulder Estimated return: 4-5 weeks.
news.ai_summary: Sydney Swans youngster Noah Howes is sidelined for 4-5 weeks with a shoulder injury, making him an immediate trade-out for fantasy coaches holding him.

news.headline: Henry Smith — OUT: Foot (3 weeks)
news.body: Henry Smith has been ruled OUT with a foot Estimated return: 3 weeks. Status updated: ETA 4-5 weeks → 3 weeks.
news.ai_summary: Brisbane Lions ruckman Henry Smith is OUT for 3 weeks with a foot injury, with his return timeline trimmed from 4-5 weeks down to 3. Fantasy coaches relying on him as ruck cover should pivot now.

news.headline: R13 preview: Race for Wildcard spots heats up, Big Freeze is back
news.body: Essendon and Carlton will meet on King's Birthday eve ahead of Melbourne and Collingwood's traditional blockbuster on Monday.
news.ai_summary: Round 13 fantasy watch: Adelaide's Josh Rachele looks set for more midfield time with Izak Rankine sidelined — he averaged 23 disposals and 4.7 clearances across the opening six rounds and is worth a look as a POD. Geelong's Connor O'Sullivan continues to firm as a cheap defender, leading the league for one-on-one wins in his third season. Hawthorn's James Sicily returned to form with 33 disposals and 13 intercepts against St Kilda, a strong sign for owners ahead of the Western Bulldogs clash and the Hawks' upcoming bye. North Melbourne's George Wardlaw (contract extension, 13 disposals and five score involvements vs Gold Coast) remains a hold, while Fremantle's Josh Treacy (28 goals already) is tracking past his career-best 45.

news.headline: Judson Clarke — OUT: ACL (2-5 weeks)
news.body: Judson Clarke has been ruled OUT with a acl Estimated return: 2-5 weeks. Status updated: ETA 3-6 weeks → 2-5 weeks.
news.ai_summary: Richmond Tigers small forward Judson Clarke is OUT with an ACL injury, now expected to return in 2-5 weeks (down from 3-6). Avoid as a fantasy option until his recovery firms up.

news.headline: Taj Hotton — TBC: Hip (Test)
news.body: Taj Hotton is in doubt with a hip Estimated return: Test. Status updated: Status INJURY TBC → TBC; Richmond → Hip.
news.change_desc: Status INJURY TBC → TBC; Richmond → Hip
news.ai_summary: Richmond's Taj Hotton is in doubt with a hip injury and faces a fitness test, leaving fantasy coaches to monitor his status closely before locking him in.

news.headline: Dees' woes, Roos' big tests: How have clubs who sold home games interstate fared?
news.body: Nathan Schmook analyses how clubs who have sold home games interstate have fared.
news.ai_summary: No direct fantasy selection, injury or price implications are detailed in this article, which focuses on the financial and on-field impact of clubs selling home games interstate. Coaches should note North Melbourne (Alastair Clarkson) plays back-to-back "home" games in WA against Fremantle and West Coast, a venue/travel factor worth weighing for Roos, Dockers and Eagles assets. Melbourne (Steven King) extended its poor Alice Springs record to 4-8 after losing to GWS, while Richmond heads to Hobart's Ninja Stadium from round 14 to face Brisbane — relevant context for matchup-based trades involving Demons, Tigers and Lions players.

news.headline: They couldn't, could they? How Blues could still be a finals contender
news.body: The Wildcard Round has opened up all kinds of possibilities, and a resurgent Carlton could take full advantage.
news.ai_summary: Carlton has won three straight under interim coach Josh Fraser to sit 4-8, with upcoming fixtures against Essendon, West Coast and Richmond offering fantasy coaches reason to back in Blues premiums for scoring runs. Historically 11-12 wins sneaks a top-10 spot, meaning Carlton's run home keeps the likes of Patrick Cripps, Sam Walsh and George Hewett relevant as captaincy and trade-in targets. With only one interstate trip left (Greater Western Sydney in Round 15) and a Round 14 bye to navigate, timing trades around Carlton's bye will be critical.

news.headline: The 'honest conversations' Swans tall has as domination creates selection conundrum
news.body: Hayden McLean has had to be patient this season, but the Swan hopes more chances are ahead after Sydney's four talls dominated.
news.ai_summary: Hayden McLean (Sydney Swans) is a selection risk for fantasy coaches despite booting 4 goals in his 100th game, as coach Dean Cox refuses to commit to the four-tall structure alongside Charlie Curnow (8 goals), Logan McDonald (2) and Joel Amartey (1). McLean's spot was only opened by injuries to small forwards Tom Papley and Malcolm Rosas jnr, making him vulnerable to being dropped back to the VFL once they return. With Cox calling it "the hardest decision" and rotating his key forwards in and out, McLean remains a hold/avoid for fantasy purposes despite his form. Curnow's eight-goal haul reinforces his value as a premium forward option.

news.headline: Good news Blues roll on as young defender gets Rising Star nomination
news.body: Harry Dean is the Telstra AFL Rising Star nominee for round 12.
news.ai_summary: Carlton's Harry Dean has locked in his defensive role with a Round 12 Rising Star nomination, posting 15 disposals, seven intercepts, three marks and four tackles at 80% game time in the Blues' win over Geelong. Across 10 games the father-son key defender is averaging 4.3 intercepts, 4.1 marks and 4.2 spoils, making him a watchable DPP-style rookie for keeper leagues despite limited fantasy ceiling. With upcoming matchups against Essendon, GWS, West Coast and Richmond, his job security looks rock-solid under interim coach Josh Fraser. Fellow Blue Jagga Smith (Round 1 nominee) remains the other young Carlton name to monitor.

news.headline: Tiger banned for wild off-the-ball strike in VFL
news.body: Richmond's Hugo Ralphsmith has been suspended in the VFL.
news.ai_summary: Richmond's Hugo Ralphsmith has been offered a two-match VFL ban for an off-the-ball strike on Sydney's Max Geddes, delaying his AFL return after recovering from an MCL injury. With the Tigers on a bye in Round 13 and resuming against Brisbane in Round 14, Ralphsmith won't be selection-relevant until at least Round 16. Fantasy coaches holding him as a cash-cow rookie should look elsewhere given Richmond's 19-man injury list isn't enough to fast-track his recall.

news.headline: Matt Rowell: Suns gun ready to embrace another battle with Lions midfielder
news.body: Suns gun ready to embrace another battle with Lions midfielder.
news.ai_summary: Gold Coast midfielder Matt Rowell shapes as a key QClash matchup play, with Brisbane co-captain Josh Dunkley likely tagging him again — Dunkley has held Rowell to 21 disposals or fewer in five straight meetings, denting his ceiling for fantasy coaches. Rowell did manage 23 disposals and a goal in last year's semi-final loss, so he's not a hard fade, but expect a tagged role. Selection-wise, Will Graham (concussion) returns for the Suns, while Jamarra Ugle-Hagan (ankle) and Lachie Weller (hamstring) are in doubt pending Thursday's main training.

news.headline: 'Hard to agree, but I understood': How Hewett bounced back after shock drop
news.body: George Hewett says his omission from the Blues' side was a tough pill to swallow, but he knew what he had to do to get back.
news.ai_summary: George Hewett (Carlton) is back in the senior side after a one-game VFL stint, where he averaged 33.7 disposals, 8.3 clearances and 6 tackles across three reserves matches to force his recall. He's now playing a forward-mid rotation role swapping with Ben Ainsworth, hitting the scoreboard in recent weeks and starting key centre bounces in the win over Geelong. Fantasy coaches should note the reduced pure-midfield time caps his ceiling, but job security looks restored under interim coach Josh Fraser.

news.headline: Ben McKay: McQualter backs young Eagle for All-Australian nod, Bomber to stay in new role
news.body: McQualter backs young Eagle for All-Australian nod, Bomber to stay in new role.
news.ai_summary: West Coast's Reuben Ginbey firms as a keeper/defender option after a career-best 17-disposal, 11-intercept, 7-spoil, 5-intercept-mark game shutting down Nate Caddy, with coach Andrew McQualter pushing him for All-Australian. Essendon's Ben McKay will stay in his new forward/ruck role under caretaker Dean Solomon after kicking his first goal since 2022 and racking up six clearances — relevant for DPP/positional shifts and his scoring ceiling. West Coast's Milan Murdock (3 goals) and Harley Reid also caught the eye as cash-generation/POD watches. Essendon's Archer May (shoulder) was subbed out and is an injury concern.

news.headline: The struggling Saints should keep Jack forward
news.body: The Bulldogs' next gen is almost here, Sydney's forward half pressure is back plus more lessons from round 12.
news.ai_summary: Fantasy owners of Jack Silvagni (St Kilda) should brace for a permanent forward shift after his 3-goal third-quarter spark vs Hawthorn — bad news for his defensive DPP value but a potential scoring floor-lift with Max King still out. Jagga Smith (Carlton) is must-watch after 27 disposals, 6 clearances and 6 inside-50s vs Geelong, shrugging off Oisin Mullin's tag and trending as a Rising Star/keeper. Reuben Ginbey (West Coast) continues his lockdown role on talls (held Nate Caddy to 10 disposals), capping his ceiling — fade for cash generation. Fremantle's Josh Treacy, Jye Amiss and Patrick Voss (78 goals combined) remain elite forward options, while Western Bulldogs depth pieces Will Lewis and Michael Sellwood are emerging cash cows worth monitoring.
